fields.description:
## Why
Adjudication of would-block/near-block plan-review findings showed the gate UNDER-blocks: 15/20 findings
in the 0.60-0.69 band on blocking criteria were block-worthy with ZERO false-positives, and four
currently-advisory criteria (G1G2, T1, T8, E4) scored 75-100% block-worthy at their would-block slice.
The 0.70 bar strands real blocks and the blocking set omits load-bearing criteria. (E1 and T3 also scored
~75% but are NOT promoted here — no threshold cleanly separates their borderline findings; deferred.)

## What
Apply the approved per-criterion thresholds/postures in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`:
- Lower `block_threshold` 0.70 -> 0.60 for exactly: COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e.
- Keep T4 @ 0.70 (its band findings were fail-safe migrations).
- Promote to `default_posture: blocking`: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75.
- E1, T3, A1 remain advisory. No other criterion changes.

## Scope
- Edit only the routing JSON + the plan-review unit tests asserting per-criterion `block_threshold` /
  `default_posture`. No engine or aggregation changes here (the impact redesign is a separate child).
- This is a deliberate BREAKING behavioral change: plans that passed before may now BLOCK. Rollout is
  coupled to the permissive-rollout child (ship the wider set first, tune via the calibration harness);
  rollback is reverting the JSON (single-commit, trivially reversible).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` matches the table above exactly (per-criterion `block_threshold` +
      `default_posture`); a test asserts each of the 6 lowered + 4 promoted + T4-unchanged entries.
- [ ] Plan-review unit tests updated and green; `make lint` passes.
- [ ] Offline replay over the sidecar corpus confirms the SIX lowered criteria now block their
      previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings.
- [ ] Offline replay confirms the FOUR promoted criteria (G1G2/T1/T8@0.70, E4@0.75) block only their
      would-block-slice findings, and reports the resulting block-rate delta on the corpus.
- [ ] No criterion outside the approved set changes posture or threshold (asserted by test).
